summary: CC-0083 introduces a dedicated push-keystone-keys OpenBao policy granting write capabilities (create, update, read) on exactly two literal KV paths used by the Keystone operator's PushSecret CRs (kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys and .../credential-keys). The bootstrap script setup-auth.sh binds this new policy alongside the existing read-only eso-management policy on the management cluster's ESO Kubernetes auth role only; the other three cluster roles (control-plane, hypervisor, storage) are byte-for-byte unchanged. eso-management.hcl stays read-only with an updated DEVIATION comment explicitly pointing at push-keystone-keys for write access, preserving least-privilege separation. The architecture submodule documentation (architecture/docs/09-implementation/09-openbao-deployment.md) is updated with the new policy block, the read-only callout, and the Apply Policies loop. Chainsaw e2e (basic-deployment) is extended to assert PushSecret status.conditions[type=Ready].status='True' for both fernet-keys-backup and credential-keys-backup, with diagnostic catch blocks that capture `kubectl describe pushsecret` and external-secrets controller logs. All files carry SPDX Apache-2.0 headers and CC-0083 feature references.

tests_checklist.item: Chainsaw e2e assertions added for both PushSecrets (Ready=True)
Catch blocks capture diagnostic output (describe + ESO logs) on regression
Existing PushSecret existence assertion preserved alongside new status assertion
JMESPath condition assertion syntax matches sibling tests in the suite
Step renumbering applied consistently after insertion of new Step 5
Manual regression validation (task 4.4) — cannot be verified in sandbox; must be evidenced in PR description

code_quality_checklist.item: Two literal path stanzas, no wildcards, no keystone/db, no keystone/* (REQ-001, REQ-006)
DEVIATION comment in eso-management.hcl explicitly references push-keystone-keys.hcl (REQ-003)
Token policy binding for management cluster: eso-management,push-keystone-keys (REQ-002)
Other cluster roles (control-plane, hypervisor, storage) unchanged (REQ-002)
setup-policies.sh dynamic *.hcl glob picks up new file with no code change (REQ-001)
Architecture doc updated with HCL block, read-only callout, and Apply Policies loop entry (REQ-004)
Architecture doc capability verbs and path literals byte-for-byte match push-keystone-keys.hcl
SPDX header + CC-0083 feature reference present on all new/changed files
Loop refactor preserves existing log message tone and idempotence (bao write upsert)
shellcheck — not runnable in sandbox (binary unavailable)

security_checklist.item: Least-privilege: push-keystone-keys grants only create/update/read on two literal paths (no delete, no list, no wildcard)
Read/push policy separation maintained — leaked eso-management token alone cannot write
openstack/keystone/db (read-only MariaDB credentials) deliberately excluded from write scope — prevents ESO from overwriting db creds and locking Keystone out of its database
No wildcard widening of eso-management policy (would have violated separation of concerns)
Push policy bound only on management cluster — control-plane/hypervisor/storage ESO cannot write Keystone keys
No secrets handled in shell logic — token_policies binding is config-only, no in-pod secret exposure vector
Policy ordering in token_policies CSV is irrelevant (OpenBao unions grants across listed policies)

architecture_checklist.item: Mirrors established push-app-credentials.hcl / push-ceph-keys.hcl pattern (capabilities + scope discipline)
No scope creep — bootstrap loop kept; only the management-cluster branch differs
Cross-reference between eso-management.hcl and push-keystone-keys.hcl makes separation self-documenting
Architecture doc + HCL + bootstrap script all reference each other (single source of truth chain intact)
Operator PushSecret RemoteKey targets verified against policy literal paths (reconcile_fernet.go:352, reconcile_credential.go:343)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated policy logic across HCL files
Catch blocks in Step 4 and Step 5 of chainsaw test are byte-identical — minor duplication
No speculative future-proofing — only the two paths the operator actually pushes to are granted
Reuses dynamic *.hcl glob in setup-policies.sh; no new wiring code needed

fail_fast_checklist.item: PushSecret Ready=True assertion will detect the 403 regression at deploy time, not days later on rebuild
Diagnostic catch block surfaces the 403 reason from ESO controller logs in CI output
set -euo pipefail preserved in setup-auth.sh; if/else branch is local var assignment, no failure modes added

defensive_checklist.item: Token policies binding uses literal cluster equality check ('management') — no fuzzy matching
bao write upsert idempotence preserved — re-running bootstrap is safe
DEVIATION comment in eso-management.hcl prevents future reviewers from silently merging the policies

issues_found.description: Catch blocks in Step 4 (fernet) and Step 5 (credential) of tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment/chainsaw-test.yaml are byte-identical (lines 182-188 vs 201-207). Both run `kubectl describe pushsecret keystone-basic-fernet-keys-backup keystone-basic-credential-keys-backup` and `kubectl -n external-secrets logs --tail=200`. External review pattern `extract-duplicated-test-assertion-blocks-into-shared-helpers` flags this kind of copy-paste at WARNING severity. Chainsaw does not have native support for shared catch blocks across steps, so the duplication is structurally hard to avoid without merging the two assertions into a single Step (which would change the step numbering scheme already established in the file).
issues_found.fix: Either (a) leave as-is — Chainsaw idiom and only ~7 lines of duplication, or (b) merge the two PushSecret Ready=True assertions into a single step with one shared catch block, since both diagnostics already cover both PushSecrets. Option (b) reduces duplication and consolidates a single "PushSecrets sync" assertion section.
issues_found.evidence: Step 4 catch (lines 182-188): `echo "=== PushSecret describe ==="; kubectl -n openstack describe pushsecret keystone-basic-fernet-keys-backup keystone-basic-credential-keys-backup ...; echo "=== external-secrets controller logs ..."; kubectl -n external-secrets logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets --tail=200 ...` is identical to Step 5 catch (lines 201-207).

issues_found.description: Sibling Chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment-2026-1/chainsaw-test.yaml (lines 161-167) asserts only the existence of `keystone-basic-2026-1-fernet-keys-backup` PushSecret and does not assert any credential PushSecret at all. A regression that re-introduces the 403 (e.g. a future policy refactor that drops `push-keystone-keys`) would still pass the 2026-1 release variant of the basic-deployment test, defeating the regression-protection intent stated in the e2e-test-engineer user story ("caught automatically in CI rather than discovered later on a rebuild"). The plan explicitly scoped the change to the 2025.2 basic-deployment file, so this is out-of-scope tech debt rather than a defect of this PR — but worth flagging as a follow-up because the 2026.1 release suite will silently miss the same class of regression.
issues_found.fix: Open a follow-up issue to mirror the new Ready=True assertions (and credential-keys-backup PushSecret assertion) into the 2026-1 variant so the regression-protection covers all release branches. Do NOT bundle into this PR — keeps PR scope minimal per Rule 4/6.
issues_found.evidence:
tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment-2026-1/chainsaw-test.yaml lines 161-167 contain only `kind: PushSecret
metadata:
  name: keystone-basic-2026-1-fernet-keys-backup
  namespace: openstack` with no status.conditions assertion and no credential-keys-backup assertion.

suggested_improvements: Consider merging the two PushSecret Ready=True assertions into one consolidated step with a single shared catch block — reduces duplication and reads as one logical "OpenBao PushSecret sync gate".
Open a follow-up to mirror the Ready=True assertions into tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment-2026-1/chainsaw-test.yaml so the 2026.1 release variant also catches push-keystone-keys regressions.

PR description must include the manual regression-validation evidence required by task 4.4 (log excerpt showing 403 from external-secrets when push-keystone-keys.hcl is removed). This cannot be verified from the worktree alone.
next_steps: Confirm the PR description includes the task-4.4 manual regression-validation log excerpt (403 from external-secrets when push-keystone-keys.hcl is absent).
Run shellcheck on deploy/openbao/bootstrap/setup-auth.sh in CI (sandbox does not have shellcheck installed).
Optionally tighten the chainsaw test by merging Steps 4 catch and Step 5 catch into one consolidated PushSecret-sync step.
File a follow-up to apply the same Ready=True PushSecret assertions to tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment-2026-1/chainsaw-test.yaml.
